interpretation_note: OIP5 should be interpreted cautiously as a dependency candidate because proliferation-linked genes can show broad essentiality across cancer models.
EGFR is a canonical receptor tyrosine kinase oncogene; interpretation should distinguish alteration/expression support from general pathway relevance.
ERBB2 is a canonical receptor tyine kinase oncogene; strongest framing is usually alteration/expression-defined subgroup biology.
MYC is a broad proliferation-associated oncogene; dependency can be strong but often has common-essential risk.

biological_process: p53 DNA damage and apoptosis pathway
interpretation_note: TP53 is a tumor suppressor; interpretation should emphasize mutation/loss and pathway disruption rather than dependency target framing.
MGMT is best interpreted as a DNA repair/resistance-associated gene rather than a classic dependency target.
CDK1 is a core cell-cycle kinase; dependency signals are likely to carry broad essentiality risk.
CDK4 is a cell-cycle kinase; dependency signals should be checked for selectivity versus broad proliferation effects.
MKI67 is primarily a proliferation marker; dependency or expression signals may reflect tumor growth rate rather than targetability.
TOP2A is linked to proliferation and DNA topology; strong dependency may reflect broad essentiality.
AURKA is a mitotic kinase; dependency should be checked for selectivity versus general proliferation.
PDGFRA is a receptor tyrosine kinase oncogene candidate; amplification/expression subgroup context is important.
MET is a receptor tyrosine kinase oncogene candidate; subgroup alteration/expression evidence is important.
KRAS is a canonical signaling oncogene; mutation-defined subgroup context is central.
MDM2 is best interpreted through p53 pathway suppression and amplification/subgroup biology.
IDH1 is best interpreted through mutation-defined biology and metabolic pathway context.
ATRX is usually interpreted through loss-of-function and chromatin/telomere biology rather than overexpression.
VEGFA is best interpreted through angiogenesis and tumor microenvironment biology rather than tumor-cell dependency alone.
MMP9 is best interpreted through invasion, microenvironment, and remodeling biology rather than direct dependency alone.
CLDN18 is best interpreted as a lineage/subtype and therapeutic biomarker candidate in gastric cancer context.
